{{Quote|If the legends are to be believed, Andeddu's Holocron contains the secret of eternal life.|Set Harth|<ref name="DOE" />}}
The '''Holocron of Heresies''', also simply known as '''Darth Andeddu's holocron''', was a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] made by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Andeddu]]. In addition to Andeddu's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]], it most notably held the secret of [[Transfer essence|transferring one's spirit]] to another body. The holocron had the phrase "''In umbris potestas est''" written on it in [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]].

==History==
{{Quote|Magnificent, isn't it? Through this holocron, I have access to the long dead wisdom of Darth Andeddu. The wisdom and power of the ''Sith''.|Count Dooku|<ref name="REP63" />}}
[[File:Red_crystal.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Darth Andeddu's lightsaber crystal]]
Lost in the annals of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] history for many years, [[Darth Andeddu]]'s [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]] was said to have first been discovered by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|fallen Jedi]] [[Freedon Nadd]].<ref name="CSWE" /> [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] in [[980 BBY]] stole it from [[Andeddu's Keep]] on [[Prakith/Legends|Prakith]] during his [[Mission to Prakith (Darth Bane)|quest]] to obtain [[Immortality/Legends|eternal life]], battling many of Andeddu's [[Malevolence (cult)|followers]] in the process. Bane eventually had to forcibly rip the secret of essence transfer from the holocron because the [[Gatekeeper/Legends|gatekeeper]] would not give him the secret of eternal life until he passed its so-called "Games". It was stolen soon after by [[Set Harth]] from the [[Doan Royal House]].<ref name="DOE" /> Centuries later, the holocron had found its way back to [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] and [[Tol Skorr]] recovered the holocron [[21 BBY/Legends|sixteen months after]] the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]] as they attempted to gain favor with [[Dooku/Legends|Darth Tyranus]].<ref name="REP63" />

After extracting the knowledge stored in the holocron, Tyranus disassembled the [[Dark side artifact/Legends|artifact]] and removed a deep red [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystal]] that had been used in Darth Andeddu's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. He gifted the ancient crystal to Vos and then ordered him to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to [[Assassination of Viento|assassinate]] [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Viento]].<ref name="REP63" />

By [[137 ABY]], the holocron had fallen into the hands of [[Darth Krayt]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] and founder of the [[One Sith]]. Lord Krayt activated the holocron in order to learn how Andeddu had been able to cheat death, but the gatekeeper refused to counsel the "pretender" because Krayt had defied the teachings of the Ancient Sith.<ref name="LEG5" />

[[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok]] [[Mission to Prakith (Darth Wyyrlok)|took]] the holocron to Andeddu's true tomb, located within a [[Andeddu's Keep|keep]] on the planet [[Prakith/Legends|Prakith]] searching for a way to cure Darth Krayt. There, it was revealed that Andeddu's holocron was unlike regular holocrons. Not only did the holocron contain Andeddu's full intellect (possibly his spirit), but was also able to manipulate the Force.<ref name="LEG27" />

After being placed near Andeddu's decayed remains, the holocron was able to siphon off the life force energy of [[Gerlun]], a member of a [[Sith cult/Legends|Sith cult]] calling itself [[Malevolence (cult)|The Malevolence]]. The holocron used Gerlun's lifeforce to reanimate Andeddu's body. Wyyrlok's own [[Mind trick/Legends#Force_illusion|illusions]] made Andeddu lose control of his reanimated body. The Force-empowered intellect was either destroyed or retreated back into the holocron.<ref name="LEG27" />
